detail: Promote a lesson: when a doc enumerates a code-owned surface (tool lists, version headers, shipped-artifact claims), make the seam EXECUTABLE — a test diffing the doc against the source of truth (README MCP surface vs TOOL_NAMES caught a silent 14-tool drift; procedure version now pinned to PROCEDURE_VERSION). Prose sweeps find drift once; the test kills the class.

Record a correction: Phase 4 ran without its in_progress transition; the skipped ritual produced phase-less dream notes AND exposed a dead engine branch (phase tables store not_started — 'ready' is computed, so the old fallback never matched). Wrong: assume mid-flight rituals happened. Right: transition when starting work, and defaults must match the table's real vocabulary.

Add one line to docs/CROSS-HOST.md host notes: Claude Code picks up .claude/skills changes live (the clauderizer-dream skill entered the running session's roster the moment the file landed) — shipping a skill and dogfooding it in the same session is viable.
[transcript-arm] The io-discipline failure excerpt (bare open() without encoding=) suggests documenting the pinned-encoding rule. Comparator candidate from deterministic transcript mining.
